# Transcrição: 05-Why Will People Hire YOU as a Coach

- **Fonte:** 05-Why Will People Hire YOU as a Coach
- **Duração:** 08:15
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

I know we just met, but can I tell you a secret? I was so scared of starting my business because when I looked online, all I saw were bros and Barbies. And I didn't feel like I was a bro or a Barbie coach. And at the time, I didn't have an Instagram. I didn't have an email list. I didn't have the things I didn't have. I was so afraid that people would figure out that I am overweight, that I've got four kids, that I drive a minivan and not a Lamborghini. It was so terrifying to start my business. And I got so stuck in thinking that I have to find this unicorn client that's going to forgive me for being Amanda Kaufman. And I share this with you because I decided one day that the pain and the difficulty of being Amanda Kaufman with no Instagram, no tinsel in my hair, no, I mean, but being straight with you. I had like eight names on a post-it when I was getting a coaching certification that needed 10 coaching interviews to finish the certification. The pain of the way things were in my career and feeling like all I wanted to do was coach. All I wanted to do was help people feel better. And one of the things I needed the most coaching on was being cool with being me. Here's what I figured out. People basically kind of want the same stuff. They want to be fulfilled. They want to be happy. They want love. They want health. They want to be wealthy. They want to have good friendships. They want to have great family relationships. And I wanted the same things.

## [03:01 - 06:01]

And the truth is those eight names on a post-it. That was a story I was telling myself at the time because I was afraid of the risk of my work colleagues finding out that I was starting a business too early. The truth is that I didn't need a unicorn client to appreciate me for who I was. I needed to appreciate who I was and be the unicorn. I needed to let other people appreciate me, but it started with appreciating myself. I'm a mother of four. I drive a minivan. It's a very practical vehicle. I invest in my business. I invest in my family. And I invest in being a ridiculously good coach. I've worked on my personal growth and development over the years. And I know that it is hard to go through change, whether you're changing your wealth, your health, your relationships. And I know that to choose joy can sometimes be the much more difficult and also the much worthier choice. And that makes me a unicorn. People want change and they choose the coach. If you don't give yourself permission to validate yourself, if you go out there asking everybody to validate you by paying you for coaching or acknowledging you for all of their amazing wins, you're going to feel pretty empty pretty fast. People want to believe you. If you're fake, if you don't work on yourself, if you don't improve on where you were yesterday, you're going to struggle to gain a following. And if you struggle to gain that following, you're always going to struggle with your business. Leaders have leads and leaders start with self-leadership. If you're not someone that you would follow, it starts with being unique, being the unicorn, and stop looking outside of yourself for unicorns. So I want to help you with this by having you do a worksheet called your unicorn approach. And this is going to be, hopefully, some of the most fun you've had in a while. Because I want to know, where do you fall on controversial topics, particularly in the area that you coach on? Like how do you approach conflict?

## [06:01 - 08:15]

What's your stance on work-life balance? Does it exist? People buy the coach just as much as they invest in themselves. You are the top 10 experts in your field. And when you study them, what do you agree with? What do you disagree with? Can you imagine being the 11th one or number one of the 10? One way that you can be a total unicorn, absolute freak in your field, is stop looking at everybody who's already in your field. You know, when I started looking at spas and gyms and plumbing businesses for my business education company, I started getting all kinds of innovative ideas. What do people know in adjacent or even unrelated fields that could inform yours? People were asking me about Facebook advertising, and there was a way that people were doing Facebook advertising in high-ticket coaching space. And I asked myself, well, where do people have to be ridiculously good at Facebook advertising that in high-ticket coaching, people can kind of afford to be lazy? And the answer? E-commerce. E-commerce. To sell t-shirts that have a really tiny margin, you have to be ridiculously good at Facebook advertising. So I went and studied Facebook advertising for e-commerce brands. Stop trying to look like everybody else in your industry and start becoming a unicorn. People working on the same goal that so many of your competitors are working on, but show up in a completely different way, you're going to not only have a different brand, you're going to have a different experience for your clients that's unforgettable, and they're going to love you for it. We'll see you in the next lesson.
